name: Abdullah ibn Sa'd ibn Nufayl
title: Leader of the Tawwabin
shortDescription: A Shia of Kufa who, out of deep remorse for not aiding the Imam, became a leader of the Tawwabin and was martyred.
fullStory: Abdullah ibn Sa'd ibn Nufayl was a prominent figure and known Shia in Kufa. Following the martyrdom of Imam Hussain (AS), he and other Kufan elders felt immense guilt for failing to assist the Imam. They formed the "Tawwabin" (Penitents) movement, led by Sulayman ibn Surad, to either avenge the Imam's blood or die trying. During the bloody Battle of Ayn al-Warda against the Syrian army, after Sulayman and Musayyab fell, Abdullah took command and fought valiantly until he was martyred.
quote: O Allah, we failed to help the son of Your Prophet, so make our blood the price for this sin.
